name: ESSAI 2026
venue: European Summer School on Artificial Intelligence, Vienna, Austria
description: The 4th European Summer School on Artificial Intelligence (ESSAI) takes place in Vienna, Austria from 6 to 10 July 2026. It offers a week‑long program of introductory and advanced AI courses, specialized tutorials, and social events aimed at PhD students, young researchers, and senior scholars. This year’s themes focus on Bilateral AI and Digital Humanism, and the school is co‑located with the 5th ACM Europe Digital Humanism Summer School.

name: OpenEuroLLM Winter School
description: The 2026 Winter School on Multilinguality in LLM Development and Evaluation is organized by the Nordic Language Processing Laboratory and the University of Oslo and takes place at the Skeikampen conference hotel north of Oslo, Norway. It targets NLP researchers, especially those from EuroHPC consortium countries, and offers doctoral training with in‑depth technical presentations, hands‑on elements, and discussions on open‑science and multilingual large language models. The program includes expert talks, walk‑throughs of EuroHPC experience reports, and networking opportunities, and participation is subsidized with no fee besides travel and accommodation costs.

name: Probabilistic AI School
description: ProbAI School 2026 is a Probabilistic AI School taking place in Vilnius, Lithuania. The school aims to provide an inclusive educational environment covering state‑of‑the‑art machine learning and artificial intelligence topics. It welcomes enthusiasts, students, academics and industry professionals to learn and collaborate.

name: BMVA Computer Vision Summer School
description: The 29th BMVA Computer Vision Summer School will be held at Durham University in Durham, England. It offers an intensive week of lectures and lab sessions covering a broad spectrum of computer‑vision topics, delivered by leading researchers from the UK and abroad. The programme features daily oral talks and hands‑on sessions designed for students and early‑career researchers seeking advanced training in the field.

name: Oxford Machine Learning Summer School 2026
venue: Oxford ML School, Oxford, United Kingdom
description: OxML Summer 2026 offers three specialised tracks in AI theory and applications, taught by world‑renowned experts from frontier AI labs in academia and industry. The programme is hosted by Oxford ML School and can be attended in Oxford or online, allowing participants to customise their learning path. Prospective participants are invited to register interest now, with applications opening soon.

name: Robotics, Perception and Learning (RPL) Summer School
venue: KTH Royal Institute of Technology, Stockholm, Sweden
description: The RPL Summer School 2026 is a six‑day event for doctoral and postdoctoral researchers focusing on robotics, perception, and learning. It covers topics such as machine learning, computer vision, human‑robot interaction, planning, and decision making, and combines keynote talks, industry panels, and open brainstorming sessions. Held at the Happy Tammsvik resort in Stockholm, the school aims to foster new collaborations by providing a relaxed setting for participants to exchange ideas and develop concrete research leads.

name: Machine Learning Crash Course at MALGA
description: The MLCC2026 Summer School – Machine Learning Crash Course – provides an introduction to fundamental machine‑learning concepts and algorithms. It is aimed at undergraduate and graduate students, as well as professionals, and is open to participants from any university after a selection process. The five‑day in‑person program in Genoa combines theoretical lectures with hands‑on lab sessions and includes a final test consisting of notebook work and a report.

name: UK Robotics Summer School
venue: National Robotarium, Heriot-Watt University, Edinburgh, United Kingdom
description: The UK Robotics Summer School 2026 is a five‑day program held at the National Robotarium, Heriot‑Watt University in Edinburgh, United Kingdom. It offers expert‑led lectures, tutorials and industry talks covering bio‑inspired robotics, healthcare robotics, generative AI for robotics, safe AI and real‑world applications, with hands‑on experience using state‑of‑the‑art technology. The school targets master’s‑level students and industry practitioners, providing networking opportunities and a special dinner.

name: International Summer School for Advanced Studies on Biometrics, Behavior, and Vision
description: The 2026 Biometrics Summer School takes place in Alghero, Sardinia, Italy, hosted at the Hotel Dei Pini. It focuses on the impact of generative AI and large foundation models on biometric and human‑recognition technologies, covering machine learning, image understanding, signal analysis, neuroscience, robotics, and forensic science. The program is aimed at PhD students, post‑docs, researchers and professionals, offering intensive lectures by leading experts, hands‑on sessions, and the possibility of scholarships and a limited‑capacity, in‑person experience.

name: 1st Internationa School on the Cognitive, Ethical, and Societal Dimensions of AI - AIces
description: AIces 2026 is the first International School on the Cognitive, Ethical and Societal Dimensions of Artificial Intelligence, taking place in Porto, Portugal from 30 March to 2 April 2026. The programme targets graduate and postgraduate students as well as industry practitioners, offering parallel courses, a symposium, and open‑debate sessions on AI cognition, ethics, and societal impact. Participants can attend face‑to‑face or join live online, with all lectures recorded and available for 45 days after the event.

name: Winter School on Ethical, Legal, and Societal (ELS) aspects of AI and AS at Umeå University
description: The Winter School on Ethical, Legal, and Societal aspects of AI and Autonomous Systems brings together researchers and students to explore the interdisciplinary challenges of responsible AI. It offers a program of lectures, hands‑on multidisciplinary training, a poster session and a social programme to foster networking and professional exchange. The three‑day event takes place at Umeå University in Sweden from 11 to 13 March 2026.

name: Winter School on Social Robotics, Artificial Intelligence and Multimedia (SoRAIM 2026)
description: The Winter School on Social Robotics, Artificial Intelligence and Multimedia (SoRAIM’27) will be held at the Escandille centre in Autrans, in the Vercors mountains near Grenoble, France. It aims to bring together PhD students and early‑career researchers from fields such as computer vision, speech processing, affective computing and human‑robot interaction to exchange cross‑disciplinary knowledge through lectures, hands‑on sessions and informal networking. The program combines theoretical and practical training with opportunities for outdoor activities, fostering new collaborations at the intersection of social AI, robotics and multimedia.

name: Northern Lights Deep Learning Winter School
venue: UiT The Arctic University of Norway, Tromsø, Norway
description: The 2027 Winter School is part of the Northern Lights Deep Learning Conference and will be held in Tromsø, Norway. It focuses on deep learning techniques, covering topics such as image classification, object detection, segmentation, time‑series prediction, and speech recognition. The school targets researchers and practitioners who wish to learn about state‑of‑the‑art methods and foster collaborations within the deep learning community.

name: Annual Nepal AI School (ANAIS)
description: The Sixth Annual Nepal AI School (ANAIS) is an 11‑day intensive program offering AI lectures, labs, and project work. It targets senior undergraduates, graduate students, PhD candidates, faculty members, and industry professionals from around the world. The school brings together over 500 in‑person participants at Swostishree Gurukul in Kathmandu to explore fundamentals and cutting‑edge AI methods.

name: EEML Summer School
description: The Eastern European Machine Learning Summer School takes place in Cetinje, Montenegro from 27 July to 1 August 2026. It features lectures and tutorials by leading researchers from institutions such as Google DeepMind, MIT, University of Oxford, and others, covering recent advances in machine learning. The program is aimed at students and early‑career researchers interested in deep learning and AI, with networking opportunities and a selection process already completed.

name: Mediterranean Summer School
description: The Mediterranean Machine Learning (M2L) Summer School is a five‑day program offering keynotes, lectures, and hands‑on sessions led by international AI experts. It is aimed at doctoral and outstanding master students, as well as academics and practitioners, with a focus on the Mediterranean region. The curriculum covers topics such as computer vision, natural language processing, generative models, reinforcement learning, and ethics in machine learning, and includes networking and optional poster presentations.

name: Summer School on Multimodal Foundation Models and Generative AI
venue: Institut National des Postes et Télécommunications, Rabat, Morocco
description: The 2nd edition of the AI Summer School on Multimodal Foundation Models and Generative AI is a five‑day immersive program jointly organized by MoroccoAI, INPT, IMT Nord Europe, and Soft Centre. It targets master, Ph.D., post‑doc, and early‑career researchers as well as industry professionals, offering state‑of‑the‑art lectures, hands‑on labs, and project mentorship on multimodal and generative AI technologies. Participants will also benefit from networking events and interactions with leading AI scientists to build a community of future AI leaders in Morocco.

name: AthNLP Summer School 2026
description: The Athens Natural Language Processing Summer School 2026 is a week‑long program held at the NCSR Demokritos campus in Athens, Greece. It brings together researchers and students to explore state‑of‑the‑art NLP methods through lectures, hands‑on tutorials, and related activities. The school is jointly organized by RC Athena, NCSR Demokritos, Athens University of Economics and Business, Heriot‑Watt University, Archimedes Unit/Athena RC, and the ELLIS‑University of Manchester network.

name: Advanced Artificial Intelligence: GenAI & Deep Unsupervised Learning
venue: Lady Margaret Hall, Oxford, United Kingdom
description: The Advanced Artificial Intelligence and Machine Learning summer school focuses on generative AI and deep unsupervised learning. It provides theoretical foundations and hands‑on experience with clustering, GANs, self‑supervised and energy‑based models, targeting STEM students with intermediate AI knowledge. The intensive residential (or online) program runs from 29 June to 17 July 2026 at Lady Margaret Hall, Oxford, offering career‑relevant skills and preparation for graduate research.

name: AI4Science Summer School
description: The 2nd Summer School on Advancing Scientific Discovery with AI brings together researchers, PhD students and post‑doctoral scholars to explore how artificial intelligence can accelerate scientific research. It covers topics such as machine‑learning for data analysis, AI‑driven experimental design, generative models, and the integration of AI with physical simulations, featuring hands‑on workshops using Python libraries like NumPy and PyTorch. The program aims to equip participants with practical skills and interdisciplinary insights to apply AI techniques to their own scientific investigations.

name: LUT Summer School
description: LUT Summer School is a one‑week intensive program held at LUT University’s Lappeenranta campus, offering courses on sustainability, climate action, marketing, management and the intersection of business and technology. The programme is aimed at bachelor’s and master’s students from any university, providing 3 ECTS credit courses that combine lectures, workshops, group projects and social activities in the Finnish summer. Participants benefit from expert teachers, a multicultural environment and optional online components, while experiencing Finland’s natural surroundings.
